document_purpose:
**Date:** 06/05/2026. **Status:** Active. Forward-looking only — no completed
items. For shipped capability state see `state-of-the-product.md`; for
session-by-session narrative see the per-§ change-log files at
`state-of-the-product-change-log-section-{5,8,9}.md`.
last_updated: kh-main-S262 — canonical-pipeline theme: added ID-45 (T7 full-corpus) + ID-46 (T14 absorption cleanup) to linked_tasks; Phew full-corpus note added

themes.title: Knowledge base foundations
themes.description:
The Knowledge Hub product is built on a single canonical knowledge base — entities, content items, classification metadata, embeddings, taxonomy, and the surrounding RLS / governance discipline. This theme covers the data-shape work that every other capability depends on: schema invariants, classification correctness, RLS enforcement, taxonomy curation, and the context graph (citation tracking, bid-outcome linking, win-based surfacing) that turns the KB into a learning system.

Outcome shape: every domain application (procurement, sales proposals, future) reads from one canonical KB. Classification is consistent across pipelines and surfaces; RLS prevents cross-tenant leakage; the context graph reveals which content drove which outcomes; taxonomy changes propagate cleanly via the canonical pipeline.

Scope boundary: this theme owns the data foundations themselves. Domain-specific consumers (bid drafting, eval rubrics, ingestion UI) belong to their respective themes; this one is the substrate.
themes.notes: Bid-outcome linking + win-based surfacing (backlog 136-139) sit here rather than under Procurement because they are KB-wide signals; classifier-quality items (92, 93) ship behavioural improvements to the classification pipeline; performance (132) covers embedding-cache choices that affect every KB consumer.

themes.title: Procurement (bid drafting)
themes.description:
The first domain application of the Knowledge Hub: end-to-end UK SMB bid drafting. Question routing, regeneration affordances, opportunity-feed integration, mobile-friendly bid sessions, and the procurement-template asset library (RFP / PQQ / G-Cloud / opportunity scan). This is the proof-of-value capability — if Procurement does not delight a real SMB bid team, the KB foundations are not delivering on their promise.

Outcome shape: a procurement team opens a bid, sees what changed since their last session, navigates by question posture, regenerates answers with templated instructions, and ships a competitive response without leaving the surface. Templates (RFP/PQQ/G-Cloud) are authored once and re-used; XLSX import handles Q&A ingestion; the bid lifecycle is observable end-to-end.

Scope boundary: bid-outcome linking + win-based content surfacing belong to Knowledge base foundations (KB-wide signals). This theme owns the bid-drafting UX surface and procurement-specific asset library.
themes.notes: No live Task ID in flight for the procurement-UX surface yet — the work sits on the backlog awaiting promotion. Re-evaluate at the next quarterly cadence whether to promote one or more procurement items into a Task.

themes.title: Cocoindex canonical pipeline
themes.description:
The canonical content ingestion + transformation pipeline built on cocoindex 1.0+. One declarative flow processes raw documents into extracted entities, classified chunks, and embeddings — replacing the previous patchwork of per-source ingestion scripts with an incremental, observable pipeline that handles change detection automatically. Cloud Run sidecar hosts the heavy extractor binaries (Docling, pullmd) so the main app stays lean.

Outcome shape: every ingestion route — markdown sync, .docx Q&A import, web scraping, future XLSX import — flows through cocoindex. Pipeline runs are observable; change detection is incremental; the Cloud Run sidecar autoscales with batch load; the schema-level KB foundations theme consumes pipeline outputs cleanly.

Scope boundary: this theme owns the pipeline itself. Domain-specific data shapes (entity schemas, classification labels) belong to Knowledge base foundations; ingestion-UX surfaces belong to the Ingestion UX theme.
themes.notes: Task 31 (canonical-pipeline → task-list migration) shipped in S65; that work is captured under SDLC workflow orchestration since it concerns ledger shape rather than pipeline behaviour. Phew full-corpus onboarding (ID-45 T7 + ID-46 T14): ingest complete Phew client corpus via cocoindex (all content types) and retire legacy pipeline post-reingest — per Liam S262 clarification superseding the Q&A-only framing. ID-45 soft-deps ID-42 (pullmd) if any Phew content is HTML/URL-sourced.

themes.title: AI evaluation and refinement
themes.description:
The systematic surface that proves the AI doing the work is getting better — not worse — over time. Per-touchpoint eval automation, taxonomy / model / data-model change-impact detection, CI gates that block regression, A/B testing of classification prompts, prompt rollback across all touchpoints, and a flag-pattern analysis pipeline that turns content-review + governance-review signals into prompt improvement suggestions.

Outcome shape: every change that touches an AI behaviour (prompt, model, taxonomy, data-model) runs through eval before merging. Regressions block; improvements are quantifiable; HITL flags accumulate into actionable prompt-change proposals; bid drafting + digest evaluation rubrics are first-class eval suites.

Scope boundary: this theme owns the eval + refinement loop. The AI behaviours being evaluated belong to their respective surfaces (classification quality lives in KB foundations; bid drafting lives in Procurement).
themes.notes: Phases 3 (infrastructure) + 4 (HITL refinement) + 5 (full coverage) map to the eval-infrastructure / ai-refinement / eval-coverage backlog tracks respectively. Bid drafting eval baseline (87) is the foundational dependency — without real bid data the Phase 5 bid-drafting rubric (103) cannot land.

themes.title: Ingestion UX
themes.description:
The client-non-developer ingest path. Today ingestion happens via developer-operated CLIs or one-off scripts; this theme delivers first-class UI surfaces so a client admin can ingest .docx Q&A files, XLSX bid spreadsheets, and markdown batches without engineering support. The /admin/imports surface unifies all ingest job-types behind one observable affordance.

Outcome shape: a non-developer client admin uploads source material via the web UI; sees ingest progress; gets dedup warnings + URL-already-exists guards; and the result lands in the KB via the canonical pipeline. The full E2E bid-automation aspiration (Contracts Finder integration) is the long-horizon endpoint.

Scope boundary: this theme owns the UX layer. The pipeline that processes the uploaded content belongs to Cocoindex canonical pipeline; the data shape it lands belongs to Knowledge base foundations.
themes.notes: EP3 UI Pattern E retrofit (61) is parked pending taxonomy + classifier stabilisation; EP8 Q&A .docx import (88) is the immediate next ingest UI surface; XLSX import (109) follows once .docx pattern is proven.

themes.title: MCP server and plugin
themes.description:
The Anthropic Model Context Protocol integration — Knowledge Hub exposes its KB via an MCP server consumable by Claude Desktop, Claude.ai, and any future MCP client. The plugin bundle (committed at lib/mcp/plugin-bundle.ts) packages the MCP App UIs so Claude Desktop users get rich, branded experiences alongside the tool surface. Daily-briefing and certification-status are the showcase tools.

Outcome shape: an external AI client (Claude Desktop, Claude.ai) authenticates against the KH MCP server, browses taxonomy via kb://taxonomy resources, queries content via MCP tools, and renders rich UIs via the bundled plugin. Marketing personas — sales, procurement, ingestion ops — each get bespoke prompt + tool sets.

Scope boundary: this theme owns the MCP surface. The KB content the MCP exposes belongs to Knowledge base foundations.
themes.notes: Marketing-persona H2 enhancements for the daily-briefing skill (49) is the next prompt-iteration surface; tool-schema hygiene (50) enforces strict: true + additionalProperties: false across TS + Python so MCP clients get reliable contract validation.

themes.title: Onboarding and UX polish
themes.description:
The first-session experience and the long-tail UX papercuts that turn a functional product into one users return to weekly. Onboarding overlays for the bid session and admin settings, getting-started guides for infrequent users, mobile bottom-sheet navigation, change-report digests as governance artefacts, shareable item-detail reports, and the why-change taxonomy validation discipline (required-Why whitelist for high-stakes columns).

Outcome shape: a first-time admin completes setup without help; a returning user sees what changed since their last visit; a mobile user can run a bid session; weekly digests are audit-grade artefacts; small UI affordances (cert-holder dropdown placeholder, shareable reports) reduce friction throughout.

Scope boundary: this theme owns onboarding + cross-cutting UX polish. Bid-specific UX belongs to Procurement; ingest-specific UX belongs to Ingestion UX.
themes.notes: Cert-holder dropdown UX (59) overlaps the procurement UX area but is generally applicable across browse surfaces, so it sits here; why-change taxonomy validation (140) is currently a needs_research spike — moves once research lands.

themes.title: Production hardening
themes.description:
The operational discipline that keeps a live SaaS reliable, observable, and safe. Auth flows (session duration, organisation branding, admin-controlled provisioning, multi-client allowlists), RLS auto-enable trigger coverage for DDL variants, rate limiting on non-AI routes, RSS feed auth design, retroactive RLS audits, sub-agent sandbox tightening, cost telemetry, async processing queues for large diffs, and Cloud Run / observability work.

Outcome shape: every public route is rate-limited; every table has RLS; cost is visible per workspace; large diffs do not block the UI; sub-agent sandboxes are tight by default; multi-tenant deployments have first-class auth + branding.

Scope boundary: this theme owns operational hardening of the live product. Workflow-orchestration hardening (sub-agent dispatch, worktree isolation) belongs to SDLC workflow orchestration.
themes.notes: Cloud Run buildpack equivalent (63) + non-cocoindex job migration (135) sit here rather than under the pipeline theme because they cover the operational deployment surface; refactor-large-monolithic-files (133) + skill budget reduction (134) are codebase-health items that affect maintenance velocity.

themes.title: Test infrastructure
themes.description:
The systematic surface that ensures every change is tested against real behaviour — not implementation accidents — and that the E2E coverage matches the surfaces customers actually use. Programmatic test audit (ts-morph + ast-grep sweep), test remediation across W-RE className coupling + W-RH validation guards, Playwright config audit against playwright-best-practices, full E2E suite CI migration, mobile viewport E2E runs, accessibility (axe-core) integration, and per-feature E2E coverage (batch create, intelligence admin, team / taxonomy mutations, entity merge / split, item lifecycle, auth logout, search filter persistence).

Outcome shape: every PR runs the full E2E suite; mobile + a11y are first-class assertions; programmatically-audited tests verify real behaviour (not the implementation); the per-touchpoint test coverage tracks the customer-observable surface area.

Scope boundary: this theme owns test infrastructure + E2E coverage. AI-behaviour evaluation (per-touchpoint eval, prompt A/B) belongs to AI evaluation and refinement.
themes.notes: Test-philosophy + playwright-best-practices skills already exist as references; the programmatic test audit (113) operationalises them against the live __tests__/ + e2e/tests/ corpus. Ingestion Phase 5 coverage (119) is dual-purpose — it serves both this theme and Ingestion UX; primary home is here since the work is the test, not the UI itself.

themes.title: SDLC workflow orchestration
themes.description:
The internal infrastructure that lets Liam + Claude Code ship Knowledge Hub features as a team. Workflow orchestration skill, task-executor agent + task-planner agent, implement-subtask skill, per-Task .md mirror generator, ledger mutation CLI, code-intelligence integration (gitnexus + ast-dataflow + ccc), worktree isolation hardening, task-list description discipline, fix-errors skill adaptation, session-driver-cmux. The roadmap-backlog consolidation work (Task 30 itself) sits here too.

Outcome shape: every implementation Subtask dispatches via a single skill (implement-subtask); every Task has a readable .md mirror; ledger mutations go through one CLI instead of inline-Python snippets; gitnexus / ast-dataflow / ccc are routinely consulted before edits; worktree isolation prevents cross-agent commit leaks; session start/end follows a stable shape.

Scope boundary: this theme owns the agent + ledger + worktree surface. Code-intelligence tools themselves (gitnexus, ast-dataflow libraries) are external dependencies; this theme covers their integration into the dispatch surface.
themes.notes: Largest theme by linked-Task count — reflects the substantial workflow-infrastructure work shipped during the production-readiness track. Task 25 (Roadmap §3 restructure) is cancelled and intentionally not linked. Task-view repo backlog items (83-85) sit here because the per-Task .md mirror viewer (Task 20) consumes them.

themes.title: Documentation surface
themes.description:
The user-facing documentation site (Astro + Starlight) ported from the Warp docubot project, plus the per-Task .md mirror viewer and the surrounding reference-doc curation discipline. Together they give Liam + the team a navigable docs surface that stays in sync with the JSON ledgers and the canonical specs.

Outcome shape: docs.kh.phew.org.uk hosts a searchable, navigable docs site rendered from PRODUCT.md / TECH.md / RESEARCH.md / PLAN.md specs; per-Task .md mirrors at docs/reference/tasks/ID-N.md let Liam read individual Tasks without loading the whole JSON; the Astro pipeline regenerates on commit; documentation freshness is enforced via doc-freshness CI checks.

Scope boundary: this theme owns the docs-site infrastructure and per-Task mirror. The content (PRODUCT.md / TECH.md / RESEARCH.md specs themselves) belongs to whichever theme the spec covers.
themes.notes: Task 9 is the compound delivery (Astro+Starlight + Warp docubot port + /update-docs decommission); Task 20 ships the per-Task mirror generator. Doc-freshness guard backlog item 86 is the next concrete maintenance task.
